item.title: Citadel Securities Sees Market Rally as Extreme Risks of War Ebb
item.description: The risk of a worst-case escalation in the Iran conflict is easing, setting the stage for a rally in both stocks and bonds, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: Repeating Venezuela-Blockade Move Is Far Riskier Bet in Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump is invoking his Venezuela playbook with a pledge to blockade Iranian ships. But Iran poses more complicated logistics, higher risks for American soldiers and a more resilient enemy.

item.title: Dollar Gains as Hormuz Blockade Sparks Oil Rally, Risk-Off Mode
item.description: The dollar gained after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following the weekend’s deadlock in US-Iran peace talks that pushed oil prices higher.

item.title: IMF Urges Kenya to Treat Securitized Cash as Debt Ahead of Talks
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said Kenya should treat future tax income used to fund major infrastructure projects as debt, a move that could upend President William Ruto’s spending plans.

item.title: Russia’s Oil Output Stable in March After Three Months of Drops
item.description: Russia’s crude output was virtually flat in March after three consecutive months of declines, as Ukraine’s attacks on its foe’s oil infrastructure continued to hamper seaborne exports and crude processing.

item.title: Chile’s New Government Looks to Speed Up Copper Expansion Amid Tight Supply
item.description: Chile’s new government is looking to boost copper production faster than previously projected by speeding up permitting and making regulatory changes, the nation’s top mining official said in an interview.

item.title: Record-Low Inflation Cushions Emerging Markets’ Oil Shock
item.description: Inflation has sunk to a record low in emerging markets even as it accelerates in advanced economies, signaling developing countries are better placed to respond to higher energy prices and could see a rebound in their bonds.

item.title: Strategy Funds Entire $1 Billion Bitcoin Buy With Hybrid Securities
item.description: For the first time since launching its high-yield securities in July, Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. funded its latest $1 billion weekly Bitcoin purchase entirely through the sale of its “Stretch” perpetual preferred shares.

item.title: Russia’s Crude Exports From Top Black Sea Port Remain Limited
item.description: Crude exports from Russia’s biggest Black Sea port are still limited, with its two largest berths yet to resume loadings after Ukrainian drone attacks last week.

item.title: Brazil Deposit Guarantor Holds Off on BRB Loan Amid Banco Master Risk
item.description: Brazil’s deposit insurance fund FGC is refusing to extend a loan to Banco de Brasilia SA, the lender facing financial strain due to its ties to Banco Master SA, until BRB can quantify its losses and specify how much capital it needs to survive.

item.title: Emerging Market Currencies Pare Drop as Oil Prices Retreat
item.description: Emerging-market currencies pared earlier losses on Monday as oil prices retreated, easing some of the risk-off pressure across EM assets as the deadline passed for the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to come into effect.

item.title: Life Science Firms Seek $693 Million in US IPOs as Pace Picks Up
item.description: Two health-care companies began formally marketing US initial public offerings raising as much as $693 million in aggregate, as listing activity in the once-prolific sector increases.

item.title: Billionaire’s US Banking Ordeal Fuels Her Mission to Disrupt It
item.description: Billionaire Cristina Junqueira had a hard time getting a credit card after landing in Miami last year with her husband and four kids in tow.

item.title: US Existing-Home Sales Fall to Nine-Month Low as Prices Rise
item.description: Sales of previously owned US homes fell in March to the lowest since June, showcasing the challenges facing the housing market as it heads into the key spring selling season.

item.title: Magyar Says Hungary Should Eventually Adopt the Euro
item.description: Election winner Peter Magyar said Hungary’s adoption is in the nation’s interest, adding he wasn’t able to give a specific target date for the switchover.

item.title: Pakistan Says Efforts Ongoing to Resolve Issues Between US-Iran
item.description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said efforts are ongoing to resolve the outstanding issues between the US and Iran.

item.title: US Natural Gas Futures Rebound on Oil Price Gain, Cooler Outlook
item.description: US natural gas futures rose alongside oil prices as renewed risk to energy flows out of the Persian Gulf triggered financial inflows into baskets of energy products. Weather forecasts also shifted cooler, indicating modestly higher demand for the heating and power-plant fuel.

item.title: SoftBank Group Plans Six-Part Bond Sale in Dollars and Euros
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. is speaking to investors ahead of a possible six-part dollar and euro bond sale, as the Japanese conglomerate continues with a debt raising spree across several currencies.

item.title: Wall Street Expects a Cautious Fed as T-Bill Purchases Wind Down
item.description: Wall Street strategists expect the Federal Reserve to take a slow and careful approach to winding down a program meant to help ease pressure in funding markets.

item.title: Hungary’s Election Winner Is Not Seeking Changes to Central Bank
item.description: Election winner Peter Magyar vowed to work with central bank Governor Mihaly Varga, saying Hungary needs to focus on difficult financial and economic situation right now.

item.title: US Says It Will Intercept, Divert or Capture Ships Leaving Iran
item.description: The US fleshed out plans to blockade Iranian ports, publishing a notice that said it could capture ships that contravene the measures.

item.title: Stocks Drop Ahead of Hormuz Blockade as Earnings Season Begins
item.description: US equities opened lower as optimism about a swift resolution to the Iran war faded after the failure of negotiations over the weekend, pushing up oil prices and inflation expectations, while first-quarter earnings season kicked off with disappointing report from Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Brazil’s Oncoclinicas to File for Creditor Protection on Monday
item.description: Oncoclinicas do Brasil Servicos Medicos SA said it will file for temporary court protection in Brazil on Monday as it faces mounting financial pressures.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Kicks Off Three-Part Investment-Grade Bond Sale
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is tapping the US investment-grade debt market again, kicking off a new round of fundraising as banks self-fund following the release of first-quarter results.

item.title: Dutch Watchdog Warns of Private Credit ‘Canary in the Coal Mine’
item.description: The Netherlands’ financial markets regulator warned of further turmoil in the $1.8 trillion private credit market after turmoil at some direct lenders.

item.title: Drugmaker Leo Pharma Is Said to Pick Banks for Copenhagen IPO
item.description: Drugmaker Leo Pharma AS has lined up investment banks to lead a potential initial public offering as soon as this year in Copenhagen, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Polish Rates Seen on Hold if Iran Fallout Capped, Janczyk Says
item.description: Poland will likely keep interest rates unchanged in coming quarters as inflation is set to stay within the central bank’s tolerance range despite pressures from the Iran war, according to policymaker Wieslaw Janczyk.

item.title: Convenience Store Operator Yesway Seeks $321 Million in US IPO
item.description: Yesway Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $321 million in its initial public offering, joining a flurry of deals looking to finish despite the conflict in Iran.

item.title: Hungary Foreign Minister Shredding EU Documents, Magyar Says
item.description: Peter Magyar accused outgoing Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto of destroying documents related to European Union sanctions as he pledged to put an end to the country’s cozy ties with Russia.

item.title: CATL Is Said to Consider Up to $5 Billion Share Sale After Rally
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is considering a share sale to raise up to $5 billion in Hong Kong following a stock rally, according to people familiar with the matter.
